description: Four guide-style documentation pages for Bethel ChMS: Managing members & visitors, Setting up ministries, Managing events & locations, Branch management

userStories.title: Write managing members section of members & visitors page
userStories.description: As a branch admin or editor, I want to understand how to add, edit, delete, and manage member lifecycle statuses, so I can manage my branch's membership confidently.
acceptanceCriteria: Create guides/managing-members.mdx replacing the existing placeholder
Frontmatter includes title: 'Managing members and visitors', description (one-sentence summary), sidebarTitle: 'Members & visitors'
Opens with brief intro: Bethel ChMS tracks two types of people, members and visitors, this page covers both

Info callout: 'Who can do this: Branch Admin and Editor can create and edit members and visitors. Only Branch Admin can delete. Organisation-level roles (Org Owner, Org Admin) have read-only access across all branches.'
H2 'Members' section heading

Adding a member subsection using <Steps>: required fields (First name, Last name), optional fields (Date of birth, Gender, Email, Mobile number, Address, Post code), status defaults to Newcomer automatically
Screenshot placeholder MDX comment: {/* Screenshot: Add member dialog showing required and optional fields */}
Email and mobile uniqueness subsection: unique within a branch, warning with link to existing record on duplicate
Tip callout: duplicate warning is non-blocking but two members cannot share same email or mobile
Editing a member subsection: navigate to detail page, click Edit, all fields editable except system-managed, duplicate detection re-runs on email/mobile change
Deleting a member subsection with Warning callout (Branch Admins only), explains soft delete, ministry assignments auto-closed, 30-day recovery
Note callout: attendance history preserved after deletion
Screenshot placeholder: {/* Screenshot: Delete member confirmation dialog showing active ministry assignments */}
Lifecycle statuses subsection: three statuses (Newcomer assigned on creation, Active, Inactive), manual transitions from member detail page

Accordion 'How automation rules work' with: newcomer-to-active (default 4 attendances in 90 days), active-to-inactive (default 30 days absence), configured at org level with per-branch overrides, disabled by default, enabled by org owner under Organisation > Automation
Filtering and searching subsection: searchable by first name, last name, email, mobile; filterable by status
No em dashes anywhere, use commas instead
Product name is 'Bethel ChMS' throughout
No technical implementation details (no database columns, RPC functions, RLS policies)
Uses exact UI labels, does not paraphrase button text
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: This story creates the file with the Members section only. The Visitors section is added in US-002. Read CLAUDE.md for brand/tone guidance and an existing guides/ page (e.g. guides/recording-attendance.mdx or guides/roles-and-permissions.mdx) for style reference before writing.

userStories.title: Add visitors section to members & visitors page
userStories.description: As a branch admin or editor, I want to understand how to add visitors, track their visits, convert them to members, and delete them, so I can manage walk-ins and follow up effectively.
acceptanceCriteria: Add H2 'Visitors' section to the existing guides/managing-members.mdx after the Members section
What is a visitor subsection: people who attend but are not yet members, tracked separately for follow-up and eventual conversion
Adding a visitor subsection using <Steps>: required fields (First name, Last name), optional fields (Gender, Email, Mobile number), visit count starts at 1, first visit date set automatically
Screenshot placeholder: {/* Screenshot: Add visitor dialog */}
Visit tracking subsection: visit count and last visit date update automatically on event check-in, first visit date never changes, fields are system-managed and not manually editable

Converting a visitor to a member subsection using <Steps>: Step 1 open record click Convert to Member, Step 2 dialog opens with pre-filled fields (first name, last name, email, mobile, gender), Step 3 review/edit and add additional fields (dob, address, post code), Step 4 click Convert creates member with Newcomer status and archives visitor
Info callout: attendance history is linked to new member record not migrated, history preserved and accessible from member profile
Tip callout: warns if visitor email/mobile already belongs to an existing member in same branch
Screenshot placeholder: {/* Screenshot: Convert visitor to member dialog with pre-filled fields */}
Deleting a visitor subsection with Warning callout (Branch Admins only), soft delete with 30-day recovery, attendance history preserved
No em dashes, use commas instead
Product name is 'Bethel ChMS' throughout
No technical implementation details
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: This story appends the Visitors section to the file created in US-001. Read the existing file first before editing.

userStories.title: Write setting up ministries page
userStories.description: As a branch admin or editor, I want to understand how to create ministries, assign members with roles, manage the roster, and archive or delete ministries, so I can organise my church's ministry teams.
acceptanceCriteria: Create guides/setting-up-ministries.mdx with frontmatter: title 'Setting up ministries', description (one-sentence summary), sidebarTitle 'Ministries'
Opens with brief intro: a ministry is an organisational grouping for teams, departments, or service groups within a branch, not an event
Info callout: 'Who can do this: Branch Admin and Editor can create, edit, and manage ministry rosters. Only Branch Admin can delete a ministry. Organisation-level roles have read-only access.'
Creating a ministry subsection using <Steps>: Name (required, unique within branch), Description (optional), Status (defaults to Active)
Screenshot placeholder: {/* Screenshot: Create ministry dialog */}
Assigning members subsection using <Steps>: open ministry click Add Member, search by name, select role (Leader, Assistant Leader, Member), set joined date and confirm
Info callout: member can only be assigned to ministry within their own branch
Screenshot placeholder: {/* Screenshot: Add member to ministry dialog with role selector */}
Ministry roles explained subsection: Leader (primary person responsible, may have additional portal permissions), Assistant Leader (supports leader), Member (general participant)
Tip callout: a ministry can have multiple leaders and assistant leaders
Removing a member subsection: sets leave date rather than deleting, tenure history preserved
Note callout: re-joining creates new assignment record, previous tenure preserved as separate record
Viewing the roster subsection: detail page shows active members (no leave date), shows name, role, joined date
Screenshot placeholder: {/* Screenshot: Ministry roster showing active members with role badges */}
Archiving a ministry subsection: sets status to Archived, cannot assign new members, roster and history preserved, can reactivate at any time
Tip callout: use archiving for paused ministries e.g. seasonal outreach team
Deleting a ministry subsection with Warning callout (Branch Admins only): soft-delete, auto-closes all active assignments, 30-day recovery
Note callout: consider archiving instead if temporary pause
No em dashes, use commas instead
Product name is 'Bethel ChMS' throughout
No technical implementation details
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Read CLAUDE.md for brand/tone and an existing guides/ page for style reference before writing.

userStories.title: Write events section of events & locations page
userStories.description: As a branch admin or editor, I want to understand how to create one-off and recurring events, manage occurrences, and delete events, so I can schedule my church's activities.
acceptanceCriteria: Create guides/managing-events.mdx with frontmatter: title 'Managing events and locations', description (one-sentence summary), sidebarTitle 'Events & locations'
Opens with brief intro: events represent scheduled activities like services, Bible studies, or outreach programs, each event happens at a location
Info callout: 'Who can do this: Branch Admin and Editor can create and edit events and locations. Only Branch Admin can delete. Organisation-level roles have read-only access.'
H2 'Events' section heading

Creating a one-off event subsection using <Steps>: Name (required), Description (optional), Location (required, defaults to primary location), Start date/time (required), End date/time (required, must be after start), single occurrence created automatically
Screenshot placeholder: {/* Screenshot: Create event dialog for a one-off event */}
Creating a recurring event subsection using <Steps>: same fields plus 'This is a recurring event' checkbox, pattern (Daily, Weekly, Bi-weekly, Monthly), recurrence end date (required)
Info callout: generates up to 52 occurrences at a time, additional occurrences generated automatically each week as needed
Screenshot placeholder: {/* Screenshot: Create event dialog with recurring options expanded */}
Understanding occurrences subsection: every event has one or more occurrences, attendance recorded against specific occurrence not event itself, each occurrence has own date/time/location/status
Editing a recurring event subsection: prompts 'Apply to all future occurrences?', past occurrences never modified
Screenshot placeholder: {/* Screenshot: Apply to all future occurrences confirmation prompt */}
Managing a single occurrence subsection: Reschedule (change date/time, marked Rescheduled), Change location (override default, 'Same as event' to revert), Cancel (marked Cancelled, actions disabled)
Screenshot placeholder: {/* Screenshot: Occurrence detail showing Reschedule, Change Location, and Cancel actions */}
Deleting an event subsection with Warning callout (Branch Admins only): removes event and all occurrences from view, attendance records preserved
No em dashes, use commas instead
Product name is 'Bethel ChMS' throughout
No technical implementation details
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: This story creates the file with the Events section only. The Locations section is added in US-005.

userStories.title: Add locations section to events & locations page
userStories.description: As a branch admin or editor, I want to understand how to create and manage locations, set a primary location, and know when a location cannot be deleted, so I can manage my branch's venues.
acceptanceCriteria: Add H2 'Locations' section to the existing guides/managing-events.mdx after the Events section
What is a location subsection: physical venue where branch holds events (e.g. main auditorium, community hall, outdoor space), every branch has a primary location created during setup
Creating a location subsection using <Steps>: Name (required), Address (optional), Post code (optional), Capacity (optional)
Screenshot placeholder: {/* Screenshot: Create location dialog */}
The primary location subsection: each branch has exactly one, events default to it, can change by selecting 'Set as Primary' on another location
Note callout: cannot delete primary location, first set another location as primary
Deleting a location subsection: cannot delete if linked to future events (directly or via inheritance), shows list of linked events if attempted
Tip callout: to delete a linked location, first reassign those events to a different location
Screenshot placeholder: {/* Screenshot: Delete location blocked dialog showing linked events */}
No em dashes, use commas instead
Product name is 'Bethel ChMS' throughout
No technical implementation details
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: This story appends the Locations section to the file created in US-004. Read the existing file first before editing.

userStories.title: Write branch creation and duplication sections of branch management page
userStories.description: As an org owner or org admin, I want to understand how to create new branches and duplicate existing ones, so I can expand my multi-site church network efficiently.
acceptanceCriteria: Create guides/branch-management.mdx with frontmatter: title 'Branch management', description (one-sentence summary), sidebarTitle 'Branch management'
Opens with brief intro: for org owners and org admins running multi-site church networks, create, duplicate, copy resources between branches

Info callout: 'Who can do this: Org Owner and Org Admin can create and edit branches. Only Org Owner can delete a branch. Branch Admins can edit their own branch details but cannot create or delete branches.'

Creating a new branch subsection using <Steps>: navigate to Organisation > Branches, click Create Branch, branch details (Name required unique within org, Email required), primary location (Name required, Address optional, Post Code optional, Capacity optional), Sunday Service (Start time, End time, Description optional, or skip), click Create provisions branch with location, Sunday Service event, 52 weekly occurrences
Tip callout: everything set up in one step, no need to create location or Sunday Service separately
Screenshot placeholder: {/* Screenshot: Create branch form showing branch details, location, and Sunday Service sections */}

Duplicating a branch subsection using <Steps>: intro (up to 10 copies from template), Step 1 context menu select Duplicate Branch, Step 2 choose copy count 1-10, Step 3 configure each copy (name, location, Sunday Service), Step 4 select additional events to clone, Step 5 review summary click Duplicate, Step 6 results screen with Retry Failed option
Screenshot placeholder: {/* Screenshot: Branch duplication wizard Step 2 showing branch name and location configuration */}
Screenshot placeholder: {/* Screenshot: Branch duplication results showing success/failure per branch */}

What gets duplicated subsection: Included (primary location, Sunday Service with 52 occurrences, selected events with occurrences, active ministries name/description/status only), Not included (members, visitors, attendance, user accounts, ministry member assignments)
Info callout: duplicated branches start empty of people, need to add members, invite users, assign ministry rosters separately
No em dashes, use commas instead
Product name is 'Bethel ChMS' throughout
No technical implementation details
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: This story creates the file with creation and duplication sections. US-007 adds the remaining sections.

userStories.title: Add copy resources, editing, deleting, and limits sections to branch management page
userStories.description: As an org owner or org admin, I want to understand how to copy resources between branches, edit and delete branches, and know about branch limits, so I can manage my network fully.
acceptanceCriteria: Add copying resources subsection to existing guides/branch-management.mdx using <Steps>: intro (copy events, locations, ministries from one branch to one or more targets), Step 1 context menu Copy Resources, Step 2 select targets, Step 3 choose resources, Step 4 review and confirm
Note callout: copied events use target's primary location, copied locations never set as primary, copied ministries have no member assignments
Screenshot placeholder: {/* Screenshot: Copy resources dialog showing resource selection */}
Editing a branch subsection: editable fields Name and Email, name must stay unique within org, branch admins can edit own branch

Deleting a branch subsection with Warning callout (Org Owner only, affects all data): soft-deletes branch and all associated data (members, visitors, events, locations, ministries, user accounts), must type branch name to confirm, 30-day recovery
Screenshot placeholder: {/* Screenshot: Delete branch confirmation dialog with name input */}
Maximum branches subsection: org has max based on plan, cannot create or duplicate beyond limit
Note callout: contact BethelWare Software Ltd to increase branch limit
No em dashes, use commas instead
Product name is 'Bethel ChMS' throughout
No technical implementation details
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: This story appends to the file created in US-006. Read the existing file first before editing.

userStories.title: Update docs.json navigation and validate all pages
userStories.description: As a documentation maintainer, I need all four new pages added to the docs.json navigation and validated, so users can discover and navigate to them.
acceptanceCriteria: Add guides/managing-members to docs.json navigation under the Guides group
Add guides/setting-up-ministries to docs.json navigation under the Guides group
Add guides/managing-events to docs.json navigation under the Guides group
Add guides/branch-management to docs.json navigation under the Guides group
All internal links across all four pages use root-relative paths without file extensions
All Bethel-specific terminology is explained on first use (ministry, occurrence, primary location, etc.)
Screenshot placeholders use format {/* Screenshot: [description] */} as MDX comments
No em dashes in any of the four pages
mint broken-links passes with no errors
mint validate passes with no errors
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: This is the final validation story. Read docs.json first to understand the existing navigation structure. Run mint broken-links and mint validate to confirm everything is wired up. Fix any issues found.
